// ============================================
// Photography Pixel Hub â€” Prisma Schema
// ============================================

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
}

// ============================================
// Enums
// ============================================

enum UserRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  EDITOR
  MEDIA_BUYER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  CLIENT
}

enum ProjectStatus {
  DRAFT
  IN_PROGRESS
  READY
  DOWNLOAD_ENABLED
  COMPLETED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum RetentionPeriod {
  TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS
  FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS
  SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS
  SEVEN_DAYS
  CUSTOM
}

enum VideoStatus {
  UPLOADING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  FAILED
  DELETED
}

enum ActivityType {
  CREATE
  UPDATE
  DELETE
  LOGIN
  LOGOUT
  UPLOAD
  DOWNLOAD
  VIEW
  ARCHIVE
  RESTORE
  RETENTION_CHANGE
}

enum ClientStatus {
  ACTIVE
  ARCHIVED
  BLOCKED
}

// ============================================
// Models
// ============================================

model User {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  email     String   @unique
  name      String
  password  String
  role      UserRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  avatar    String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  clients      Client[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]

  @@index([email])
  @@map("users")
}

model Client {
  id         String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId     String       @db.Uuid
  clientCode String       @unique
  name       String
  company    String?
  email      String
  phone      String?
  notes      String?
  status     ClientStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  token      String       @unique
  archivedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime      @updatedAt

  user      User       @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projects  Project[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@unique([userId, email])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([email])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([clientCode])
  @@map("clients")
}

model Project {
  id             String         @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectCode    String         @unique
  clientId       String         @db.Uuid
  name           String
  description    String?
  status         ProjectStatus  @default(DRAFT)
  retentionPeriod RetentionPeriod @default(TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS)
  deadline       DateTime?
  token          String         @unique
  archivedAt     DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime       @updatedAt

  client    Client    @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  videos    Video[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([projectCode])
  @@index([archivedAt])
  @@index([retentionPeriod])
  @@map("projects")
}

model Video {
  id              String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoCode       String       @unique
  projectId       String       @db.Uuid
  title           String
  originalFileName String
  storageKey      String       @unique
  storageBucket   String
  mimeType        String
  extension       String
  fileSize        BigInt
  duration        Int?
  width           Int?
  height          Int?
  thumbnailUrl    String?
  streamUrl       String?
  downloadUrl     String?
  status          VideoStatus  @default(UPLOADING)
  uploadedAt      DateTime?
  processedAt     DateTime?
  deletedAt       DateTime?
  createdAt       DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime     @updatedAt

  project   Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([videoCode])
  @@index([deletedAt])
  @@map("videos")
}

model Download {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  video   Video   @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  client  Client  @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("downloads")
}

model View {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  duration   Int?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  video   Video   @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  client  Client  @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("views")
}

model ActivityLog {
  id        String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId    String       @db.Uuid
  type      ActivityType
  entity    String
  entityId   String
  metadata   Json?
  createdAt DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime     @updatedAt

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([type])
  @@index([entity, entityId])
  @@map("activity_logs")
}

args.result:
## Overview I thoroughly explored the Photography Pixel Hub codebase (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, Prisma 7, PostgreSQL, RTL Arabic UI). All 24 requested files were read in full. Note: the `read_file` tool cached ~7 files as "unchanged since last read" from a prior context I couldn't see, so I recovered their contents via PowerShell `Get-Content -LiteralPath` (the bracketed paths `[id]`/`[token]` required `-LiteralPath` to avoid PowerShell wildcard errors). Below are the patterns most relevant to building a new Delivery Portal module. ## 1. Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`) **Enums:** `UserRole` (OWNER, ADMIN, EDITOR, MEDIA_BUYER, PHOTOGRAPHER, CLIENT), `ProjectStatus` (DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS, READY, DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), `RetentionPeriod` (TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS, FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS, SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS, SEVEN_DAYS, CUSTOM), `VideoStatus` (UPLOADING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, DELETED), `ActivityType` (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, VIEW, ARCHIVE, RESTORE, RETENTION_CHANGE), `ClientStatus` (ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED). **Models:** All use `String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid`, `createdAt @default(now())`, `updatedAt @updatedAt`. Key convention for public access: both `Client` and `Project` have a `token String @unique` field AND a sequential `clientCode`/`projectCode String @unique` field. Soft-delete via `archivedAt DateTime?`. `ActivityLog` (userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata Json?) provides the audit trail. `Download` and `View` are junction-style models tracking videoId/clientId/projectId + ip/userAgent. A new Delivery model should follow the same shape: uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status enum, relations to Project/Client, soft-delete `archivedAt`, and reuse `ActivityLog` for auditing. ## 2. Routes (`src/lib/constants/routes.ts`) `ROUTES` object with static strings + dynamic helpers like `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_DETAILS: (id) => /dashboard/projects/${id}` and `PUBLIC_GALLERY: (token) => /p/${token}`. `PROTECTED_ROUTES = ["/dashboard"]`, `AUTH_ROUTES = ["/login"]`, `API_ROUTES = { UPLOAD, WEBHOOKS }`. A delivery portal would add e.g. `PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token) => /d/${token}` and dashboard routes like `DASHBOARD_DELIVERIES`. ## 3. Navigation (`src/lib/constants/navigation.ts`) `NAV_SECTIONS: NavSection[]` (each `{title, items: NavItem[]}`), `NavItem = {label, href?, icon: LucideIcon, action?: "logout"}`. `NAV_FOOTER_ITEMS` for settings/profile/logout. `PAGE_TITLES` and `BREADCRUMB_LABELS` are `Record` keyed by path segment. New nav entries go in `NAV_SECTIONS`; add breadcrumb labels for any new segments. ## 4. Auth route group `src/app/(auth)/` `(auth)/layout.tsx` = centered full-screen layout (`min-h-dvh`, centered, APP_NAME heading). `(auth)/login/page.tsx` is a `"use client"` form using **react-hook-form + zodResolver(loginSchema)**, builds `FormData`, calls `loginAction(prevState, formData)` server action, handles `?redirect=` and `?reason=expired` query params via `getSafeRedirect()`, shows toasts (sonner). Pattern to reuse for any delivery login/gate page. ## 5. Public gallery route `src/app/p/[token]/` Contains `page.tsx`, `loading.tsx` (spinner), `error.tsx` (`"use client"`, `useEffect` console.error, reset button). **Critical pattern:** `page.tsx` is an `async` server component with `params: Promise<{ token: string }>`, awaits `const { token } = await params`. Currently a **stabilization stub** ("المعرض غير متاح حالياً") — no real gallery logic yet, so the Delivery Portal can establish the real token-based public-access pattern. This is the template for a `/d/[token]` delivery route. ## 6. Project details page (`src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/page.tsx`) Server component, `async params: Promise<{id}>`, calls `projectService.getById(id)` directly (not via action), `notFound()` if missing, fetches `getStatistics` + `activityService.list({entity, entityId, take})`. Uses `PageHeader` (with breadcrumbs + back button action), `DashboardCard`, `SectionTitle`, a local `StatCard` component, `ProjectStatusBadge`/`RetentionBadge`, `EmptyState`. Layout: status bar → 4-col stat grid → 2/3 + 1/3 split (info cards left, activity timeline right). The directory also has a `[id]/videos/page.tsx` subroute. **A "Deliveries" tab/section would slot into this page** alongside the Videos card, or as a new `[id]/deliveries` subroute. ## 7. Clients list page (`src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx`) Minimal: `export const metadata = { title }`, server component returning `
`. List pages are thin server shells delegating to a client `*Table` component (which owns search/filter/sort/pagination via server actions). ## 8. Shared components - **`page-header.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{title, description?, actions?, breadcrumbs?}`; auto-generates breadcrumbs from `usePathname()` using `BREADCRUMB_LABELS`. RTL-aware (`ChevronLeft` with `rtl-flip`). - **`empty-state.tsx`**: props `{icon: LucideIcon, title, description?, action?, className?, iconClassName?}` — circular muted icon + title + description + optional action. - **`confirm-dialog.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{open, onOpenChange, title, description, confirmLabel?, cancelLabel?, variant?: "default"|"destructive", onConfirm}`. Wraps Radix `Dialog` + `Button`. `onConfirm` awaited then closes. ## 9–10. Auth session & config `src/lib/auth/session.ts`: JWT via **jose** (Edge-compatible). `getCurrentUser()` → `SessionUser | null` (never throws). `requireUser()` throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ExpiredSessionError`. `requireRole(...allowedRoles: UserRole[])` throws `ForbiddenError`. `createSession(user)` signs + sets cookie; `destroySession()` deletes it. `SessionUser = {id, email, name, role, avatar}`. `src/lib/auth/config.ts`: cookie name `pph-session`, alg HS256, maxAge 7 days, `cookieOptions = {httpOnly, sameSite:"lax", secure: prod-only, path:"/", maxAge}`. Rate limit (5 attempts/min, 15-min lockout). **`DUMMY_HASH`** bcrypt constant for timing-attack prevention on missing users. ## 11–12. Utils `src/lib/utils/format.ts`: `formatBytes(bytes, decimals=2)`, `formatDate(date, locale="ar-SA", opts)` (Arabic long date), `formatDateTime`, `formatNumber` — all use `Intl` with `ar-SA`. `src/lib/utils/index.ts`: re-exports `cn`, format fns; **`generateToken(length=32)`** uses `crypto.getRandomValues` (browser+Node) over `[A-Za-z0-9]` — reuse for delivery tokens. Also `sleep(ms)`. ## 13–14. Activity logging (service + repository) `activityService.log({userId, type: ActivityType, entity, entityId, metadata?})` → `activityRepository.create(...)`. `activityService.list({userId?, type?, entity?, entityId?, skip?, take?})` builds a `where` and orders by `createdAt desc`. `activityRepository` is the ONLY Prisma-touching layer; exports `ActivityLogWithRelations`, `*CreateInput`, `*WhereInput` types from `Prisma.*`. Methods: `findById, create, findMany({where,skip,take,orderBy}), count`. ## 15. Server action pattern (`src/actions/projects/get-project-details.ts`) `"use server"`; `const user = await getCurrentUser(); if(!user) return null;` (graceful null, no throw); calls `projectService`; returns plain serializable object `{project, statistics, timeline}`. Actions are the server→client boundary; services hold logic. ## 16. Video player (`src/components/player/video-player.tsx`) `"use client"` full-screen modal overlay (`fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/90`). Props `{src, title?, poster?, onClose?, autoPlay?}`. Custom controls: play/pause, seek bar, volume/mute, playback speed (0.5–2x), fullscreen. Uses `useRef` + event listeners. Reusable for delivery video playback. ## 17. Storage service (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`) Facade pattern: `StorageProvider` interface + `CloudflareR2Provider` (active), swappable via `STORAGE_CONFIG.PROVIDER`. Singleton `storageService` exposes: `upload, replace, delete, exists, getSignedUrl, getDownloadUrl, getStreamingUrl, generateUploadUrl`. Re-exported types `StorageProvider, UploadParams, SignedUrlOptions`. Deliveries should use `getDownloadUrl`/`getStreamingUrl` for secure token-based file access. ## 18. Project types (`src/types/project.ts`) App-level types: `ProjectWithRelations`, `ProjectTableRow` (flat display row with nested `client`), `PaginatedProjects {items, total, page, pageSize, totalPages}`, `ProjectStatistics {videos, views, downloads, storageSize}`, `ProjectDetails`. Mirror this for `Delivery` types. ## 19. Validations (`src/lib/validations/project.ts`) Zod. Enum schemas (`projectStatusSchema`, `retentionPeriodSchema`, `projectFilterSchema`, `projectSortSchema`). `createProjectSchema`/`updateProjectSchema` (`z.object` with `nameSchema` from `./auth`, `z.coerce.date()`, Arabic `.max()` messages). `projectQuerySchema` with `z.coerce.number()` defaults + `refine` for page sizes `[10,25,50,100]`. Types via `z.infer`. Reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth` and the query-schema pattern for deliveries. ## 20. Repository pattern (`src/repositories/project.repository.ts`) ONLY Prisma layer. Exports `ProjectWithRelations = Prisma.ProjectGetPayload<{include:{client,videos}}>`, `*CreateInput`/`*UpdateInput` (Unchecked), `*WhereInput`. Private `buildWhere`/`buildOrderBy`/`statusFromFilter` helpers. Methods: `findById, findByProjectCode, findByToken, create, update, softDelete (status=ARCHIVED+archivedAt), restore, delete (hard, tests only), findMany (parallel findMany+count), count, findLatestProjectCode, getStatistics (parallel video sizes + view/download counts), findAllForExport`. **`findByToken`** is the key method to mirror for public delivery access. ## 21. Service pattern (`src/services/project.service.ts`) Business logic, calls repositories only. `generateUniqueProjectCode()` uses `prisma.$transaction` to read latest `projectCode`, parse `PR-(\d+)`, increment, format `PR-000001`. `normalizeInput` trims. `create` generates code+`generateToken(32)`, creates, then **conditional activity logging** via `options.actorId` (CREATE, and UPDATE logs RETENTION_CHANGE when changed). `archive`/`restore`/`delete` (alias). `list`/`count` map filter→status. `getTimeline` delegates to `activityService.list`. `exportCsv` builds Arabic CSV with BOM. Reuse the `{actorId}` options + sequential-code-via-transaction + token-generation pattern for deliveries. ## 22. Video grid (`src/components/uploads/video-grid.tsx`) `"use client"` grid with `useTransition`. State: `data, search, filter, sort, page, pageSize` + targets for delete/restore/player. `fetchData` calls `getUploadsAction`. **`useMemo` (not useEffect) triggers `fetchData`** on dependency change; debounced search via `setTimeout` in a `useMemo`. Toolbar: search input (RTL `ps-9`), filter tablist, sort select. Grid: `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`, skeleton loaders, `EmptyState`, status badges, thumbnail→`handlePlay` fetches signed URLs then opens `VideoPlayer`. Pagination with page-size select + ChevronRight/Left (RTL). `ConfirmDialog` for delete/restore with sonner toasts. Template for a delivery file grid. ## 23. bcryptjs — AVAILABLE ✓ `package.json`: `"bcryptjs": "^3.0.3"` (dependency) + `"@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6"` (devDependency). Ready for password-hashing on password-protected deliveries. The `DUMMY_HASH` constant in `auth/config.ts` confirms the `bcrypt.compare` timing-attack pattern is already in use (likely in the login action). ## 24. Root layout (`src/app/layout.tsx`) ``, Cairo (Arabic) + Inter fonts. Providers nested: `ThemeProvider` (attribute="class", defaultTheme="dark", enableSystem) → `SessionProvider initialUser={user}` (user fetched server-side via `getCurrentUser()`) → `QueryProvider` (TanStack) → children + `Toaster` (sonner, top-center, richColors, rtl). `metadata` uses APP_NAME/APP_DESCRIPTION/APP_LOCALE/APP_URL from `@/lib/constants`. New public delivery routes inherit this layout automatically. ## Bonus findings - `middleware.ts` exists at project root (protects `/dashboard` per PROTECTED_ROUTES) — public `/d/[token]` routes must be excluded like `/p/[token]` and `/login`. - Project details dir has subroute `[id]/videos/page.tsx` — a `[id]/deliveries` subroute would follow the same convention. - The `(auth)` route group is a Next.js route group (parens, no URL segment) for shared centered layout — useful if deliveries need a separate gate/login layout. - `SessionProvider` receives `initialUser` from the server, so client components can read the current user without re-fetching. ## Architectural layering summary (for new Delivery module) `prisma/schema.prisma` → `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` (only Prisma layer) → `src/services/delivery.service.ts` (logic, token+code generation, activity logging via `{actorId}`) → `src/actions/deliveries/*.ts` (`"use server"`, `getCurrentUser` guard) → `src/components/deliveries/*` (client UI: table/grid/forms) → `src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/deliveries/page.tsx` (admin tab) + `src/app/d/[token]/page.tsx` (public portal). Validations in `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema`), types in `src/types/delivery.ts`, routes/nav in constants, and bcryptjs for any password-gated deliveries.
args.next_steps: Build the Delivery Portal module following the established layered pattern: (1) Add `Delivery` model + `DeliveryStatus`/`DeliveryItem` enums to `prisma/schema.prisma` mirroring the Project shape (uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status, relations to Project/Client, `archivedAt` soft-delete). (2) Create `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` with `findByToken`/`findById`/`findMany`/`create`/`softDelete` mirroring project.repository. (3) Create `src/services/delivery.service.ts` with sequential-code transaction + `generateToken(32)` + `{actorId}` activity logging. (4) Add `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth`) and `src/types/delivery.ts`. (5) Add routes to `routes.ts` (`PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token)=>/d/${token}`, dashboard delivery routes) and a nav entry + breadcrumb labels in `navigation.ts`. (6) Ensure `middleware.ts` excludes the new `/d` public path (like `/p`). (7) Add server actions in `src/actions/deliveries/`, a `[id]/deliveries` admin tab under the project details page, and the public `/d/[token]/page.tsx` (with loading.tsx + error.tsx) reusing `VideoPlayer`, `storageService.getDownloadUrl/getStreamingUrl`, and bcryptjs for password-gated deliveries.

Task completed. Output: Status: success Result: ## Overview I thoroughly explored the Photography Pixel Hub codebase (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, Prisma 7, PostgreSQL, RTL Arabic UI). All 24 requested files were read in full. Note: the `read_file` tool cached ~7 files as "unchanged since last read" from a prior context I couldn't see, so I recovered their contents via PowerShell `Get-Content -LiteralPath` (the bracketed paths `[id]`/`[token]` required `-LiteralPath` to avoid PowerShell wildcard errors). Below are the patterns most relevant to building a new Delivery Portal module. ## 1. Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`) **Enums:** `UserRole` (OWNER, ADMIN, EDITOR, MEDIA_BUYER, PHOTOGRAPHER, CLIENT), `ProjectStatus` (DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS, READY, DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), `RetentionPeriod` (TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS, FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS, SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS, SEVEN_DAYS, CUSTOM), `VideoStatus` (UPLOADING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, DELETED), `ActivityType` (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, VIEW, ARCHIVE, RESTORE, RETENTION_CHANGE), `ClientStatus` (ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED). **Models:** All use `String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid`, `createdAt @default(now())`, `updatedAt @updatedAt`. Key convention for public access: both `Client` and `Project` have a `token String @unique` field AND a sequential `clientCode`/`projectCode String @unique` field. Soft-delete via `archivedAt DateTime?`. `ActivityLog` (userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata Json?) provides the audit trail. `Download` and `View` are junction-style models tracking videoId/clientId/projectId + ip/userAgent. A new Delivery model should follow the same shape: uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status enum, relations to Project/Client, soft-delete `archivedAt`, and reuse `ActivityLog` for auditing. ## 2. Routes (`src/lib/constants/routes.ts`) `ROUTES` object with static strings + dynamic helpers like `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_DETAILS: (id) => /dashboard/projects/${id}` and `PUBLIC_GALLERY: (token) => /p/${token}`. `PROTECTED_ROUTES = ["/dashboard"]`, `AUTH_ROUTES = ["/login"]`, `API_ROUTES = { UPLOAD, WEBHOOKS }`. A delivery portal would add e.g. `PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token) => /d/${token}` and dashboard routes like `DASHBOARD_DELIVERIES`. ## 3. Navigation (`src/lib/constants/navigation.ts`) `NAV_SECTIONS: NavSection[]` (each `{title, items: NavItem[]}`), `NavItem = {label, href?, icon: LucideIcon, action?: "logout"}`. `NAV_FOOTER_ITEMS` for settings/profile/logout. `PAGE_TITLES` and `BREADCRUMB_LABELS` are `Record` keyed by path segment. New nav entries go in `NAV_SECTIONS`; add breadcrumb labels for any new segments. ## 4. Auth route group `src/app/(auth)/` `(auth)/layout.tsx` = centered full-screen layout (`min-h-dvh`, centered, APP_NAME heading). `(auth)/login/page.tsx` is a `"use client"` form using **react-hook-form + zodResolver(loginSchema)**, builds `FormData`, calls `loginAction(prevState, formData)` server action, handles `?redirect=` and `?reason=expired` query params via `getSafeRedirect()`, shows toasts (sonner). Pattern to reuse for any delivery login/gate page. ## 5. Public gallery route `src/app/p/[token]/` Contains `page.tsx`, `loading.tsx` (spinner), `error.tsx` (`"use client"`, `useEffect` console.error, reset button). **Critical pattern:** `page.tsx` is an `async` server component with `params: Promise<{ token: string }>`, awaits `const { token } = await params`. Currently a **stabilization stub** ("المعرض غير متاح حالياً") — no real gallery logic yet, so the Delivery Portal can establish the real token-based public-access pattern. This is the template for a `/d/[token]` delivery route. ## 6. Project details page (`src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/page.tsx`) Server component, `async params: Promise<{id}>`, calls `projectService.getById(id)` directly (not via action), `notFound()` if missing, fetches `getStatistics` + `activityService.list({entity, entityId, take})`. Uses `PageHeader` (with breadcrumbs + back button action), `DashboardCard`, `SectionTitle`, a local `StatCard` component, `ProjectStatusBadge`/`RetentionBadge`, `EmptyState`. Layout: status bar → 4-col stat grid → 2/3 + 1/3 split (info cards left, activity timeline right). The directory also has a `[id]/videos/page.tsx` subroute. **A "Deliveries" tab/section would slot into this page** alongside the Videos card, or as a new `[id]/deliveries` subroute. ## 7. Clients list page (`src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx`) Minimal: `export const metadata = { title }`, server component returning `
`. List pages are thin server shells delegating to a client `*Table` component (which owns search/filter/sort/pagination via server actions). ## 8. Shared components - **`page-header.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{title, description?, actions?, breadcrumbs?}`; auto-generates breadcrumbs from `usePathname()` using `BREADCRUMB_LABELS`. RTL-aware (`ChevronLeft` with `rtl-flip`). - **`empty-state.tsx`**: props `{icon: LucideIcon, title, description?, action?, className?, iconClassName?}` — circular muted icon + title + description + optional action. - **`confirm-dialog.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{open, onOpenChange, title, description, confirmLabel?, cancelLabel?, variant?: "default"|"destructive", onConfirm}`. Wraps Radix `Dialog` + `Button`. `onConfirm` awaited then closes. ## 9–10. Auth session & config `src/lib/auth/session.ts`: JWT via **jose** (Edge-compatible). `getCurrentUser()` → `SessionUser | null` (never throws). `requireUser()` throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ExpiredSessionError`. `requireRole(...allowedRoles: UserRole[])` throws `ForbiddenError`. `createSession(user)` signs + sets cookie; `destroySession()` deletes it. `SessionUser = {id, email, name, role, avatar}`. `src/lib/auth/config.ts`: cookie name `pph-session`, alg HS256, maxAge 7 days, `cookieOptions = {httpOnly, sameSite:"lax", secure: prod-only, path:"/", maxAge}`. Rate limit (5 attempts/min, 15-min lockout). **`DUMMY_HASH`** bcrypt constant for timing-attack prevention on missing users. ## 11–12. Utils `src/lib/utils/format.ts`: `formatBytes(bytes, decimals=2)`, `formatDate(date, locale="ar-SA", opts)` (Arabic long date), `formatDateTime`, `formatNumber` — all use `Intl` with `ar-SA`. `src/lib/utils/index.ts`: re-exports `cn`, format fns; **`generateToken(length=32)`** uses `crypto.getRandomValues` (browser+Node) over `[A-Za-z0-9]` — reuse for delivery tokens. Also `sleep(ms)`. ## 13–14. Activity logging (service + repository) `activityService.log({userId, type: ActivityType, entity, entityId, metadata?})` → `activityRepository.create(...)`. `activityService.list({userId?, type?, entity?, entityId?, skip?, take?})` builds a `where` and orders by `createdAt desc`. `activityRepository` is the ONLY Prisma-touching layer; exports `ActivityLogWithRelations`, `*CreateInput`, `*WhereInput` types from `Prisma.*`. Methods: `findById, create, findMany({where,skip,take,orderBy}), count`. ## 15. Server action pattern (`src/actions/projects/get-project-details.ts`) `"use server"`; `const user = await getCurrentUser(); if(!user) return null;` (graceful null, no throw); calls `projectService`; returns plain serializable object `{project, statistics, timeline}`. Actions are the server→client boundary; services hold logic. ## 16. Video player (`src/components/player/video-player.tsx`) `"use client"` full-screen modal overlay (`fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/90`). Props `{src, title?, poster?, onClose?, autoPlay?}`. Custom controls: play/pause, seek bar, volume/mute, playback speed (0.5–2x), fullscreen. Uses `useRef` + event listeners. Reusable for delivery video playback. ## 17. Storage service (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`) Facade pattern: `StorageProvider` interface + `CloudflareR2Provider` (active), swappable via `STORAGE_CONFIG.PROVIDER`. Singleton `storageService` exposes: `upload, replace, delete, exists, getSignedUrl, getDownloadUrl, getStreamingUrl, generateUploadUrl`. Re-exported types `StorageProvider, UploadParams, SignedUrlOptions`. Deliveries should use `getDownloadUrl`/`getStreamingUrl` for secure token-based file access. ## 18. Project types (`src/types/project.ts`) App-level types: `ProjectWithRelations`, `ProjectTableRow` (flat display row with nested `client`), `PaginatedProjects {items, total, page, pageSize, totalPages}`, `ProjectStatistics {videos, views, downloads, storageSize}`, `ProjectDetails`. Mirror this for `Delivery` types. ## 19. Validations (`src/lib/validations/project.ts`) Zod. Enum schemas (`projectStatusSchema`, `retentionPeriodSchema`, `projectFilterSchema`, `projectSortSchema`). `createProjectSchema`/`updateProjectSchema` (`z.object` with `nameSchema` from `./auth`, `z.coerce.date()`, Arabic `.max()` messages). `projectQuerySchema` with `z.coerce.number()` defaults + `refine` for page sizes `[10,25,50,100]`. Types via `z.infer`. Reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth` and the query-schema pattern for deliveries. ## 20. Repository pattern (`src/repositories/project.repository.ts`) ONLY Prisma layer. Exports `ProjectWithRelations = Prisma.ProjectGetPayload<{include:{client,videos}}>`, `*CreateInput`/`*UpdateInput` (Unchecked), `*WhereInput`. Private `buildWhere`/`buildOrderBy`/`statusFromFilter` helpers. Methods: `findById, findByProjectCode, findByToken, create, update, softDelete (status=ARCHIVED+archivedAt), restore, delete (hard, tests only), findMany (parallel findMany+count), count, findLatestProjectCode, getStatistics (parallel video sizes + view/download counts), findAllForExport`. **`findByToken`** is the key method to mirror for public delivery access. ## 21. Service pattern (`src/services/project.service.ts`) Business logic, calls repositories only. `generateUniqueProjectCode()` uses `prisma.$transaction` to read latest `projectCode`, parse `PR-(\d+)`, increment, format `PR-000001`. `normalizeInput` trims. `create` generates code+`generateToken(32)`, creates, then **conditional activity logging** via `options.actorId` (CREATE, and UPDATE logs RETENTION_CHANGE when changed). `archive`/`restore`/`delete` (alias). `list`/`count` map filter→status. `getTimeline` delegates to `activityService.list`. `exportCsv` builds Arabic CSV with BOM. Reuse the `{actorId}` options + sequential-code-via-transaction + token-generation pattern for deliveries. ## 22. Video grid (`src/components/uploads/video-grid.tsx`) `"use client"` grid with `useTransition`. State: `data, search, filter, sort, page, pageSize` + targets for delete/restore/player. `fetchData` calls `getUploadsAction`. **`useMemo` (not useEffect) triggers `fetchData`** on dependency change; debounced search via `setTimeout` in a `useMemo`. Toolbar: search input (RTL `ps-9`), filter tablist, sort select. Grid: `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`, skeleton loaders, `EmptyState`, status badges, thumbnail→`handlePlay` fetches signed URLs then opens `VideoPlayer`. Pagination with page-size select + ChevronRight/Left (RTL). `ConfirmDialog` for delete/restore with sonner toasts. Template for a delivery file grid. ## 23. bcryptjs — AVAILABLE ✓ `package.json`: `"bcryptjs": "^3.0.3"` (dependency) + `"@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6"` (devDependency). Ready for password-hashing on password-protected deliveries. The `DUMMY_HASH` constant in `auth/config.ts` confirms the `bcrypt.compare` timing-attack pattern is already in use (likely in the login action). ## 24. Root layout (`src/app/layout.tsx`) ``, Cairo (Arabic) + Inter fonts. Providers nested: `ThemeProvider` (attribute="class", defaultTheme="dark", enableSystem) → `SessionProvider initialUser={user}` (user fetched server-side via `getCurrentUser()`) → `QueryProvider` (TanStack) → children + `Toaster` (sonner, top-center, richColors, rtl). `metadata` uses APP_NAME/APP_DESCRIPTION/APP_LOCALE/APP_URL from `@/lib/constants`. New public delivery routes inherit this layout automatically. ## Bonus findings - `middleware.ts` exists at project root (protects `/dashboard` per PROTECTED_ROUTES) — public `/d/[token]` routes must be excluded like `/p/[token]` and `/login`. - Project details dir has subroute `[id]/videos/page.tsx` — a `[id]/deliveries` subroute would follow the same convention. - The `(auth)` route group is a Next.js route group (parens, no URL segment) for shared centered layout — useful if deliveries need a separate gate/login layout. - `SessionProvider` receives `initialUser` from the server, so client components can read the current user without re-fetching. ## Architectural layering summary (for new Delivery module) `prisma/schema.prisma` → `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` (only Prisma layer) → `src/services/delivery.service.ts` (logic, token+code generation, activity logging via `{actorId}`) → `src/actions/deliveries/*.ts` (`"use server"`, `getCurrentUser` guard) → `src/components/deliveries/*` (client UI: table/grid/forms) → `src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/deliveries/page.tsx` (admin tab) + `src/app/d/[token]/page.tsx` (public portal). Validations in `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema`), types in `src/types/delivery.ts`, routes/nav in constants, and bcryptjs for any password-gated deliveries. Next steps: Build the Delivery Portal module following the established layered pattern: (1) Add `Delivery` model + `DeliveryStatus`/`DeliveryItem` enums to `prisma/schema.prisma` mirroring the Project shape (uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status, relations to Project/Client, `archivedAt` soft-delete). (2) Create `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` with `findByToken`/`findById`/`findMany`/`create`/`softDelete` mirroring project.repository. (3) Create `src/services/delivery.service.ts` with sequential-code transaction + `generateToken(32)` + `{actorId}` activity logging. (4) Add `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth`) and `src/types/delivery.ts`. (5) Add routes to `routes.ts` (`PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token)=>/d/${token}`, dashboard delivery routes) and a nav entry + breadcrumb labels in `navigation.ts`. (6) Ensure `middleware.ts` excludes the new `/d` public path (like `/p`). (7) Add server actions in `src/actions/deliveries/`, a `[id]/deliveries` admin tab under the project details page, and the public `/d/[token]/page.tsx` (with loading.tsx + error.tsx) reusing `VideoPlayer`, `storageService.getDownloadUrl/getStreamingUrl`, and bcryptjs for password-gated deliveries.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

11. **Format utils**: formatBytes, formatDate (ar-SA, Arabic), formatDateTime, formatNumber.
12. **generateToken**: crypto.getRandomValues, 32 chars default, alphanumeric.
13. **Activity service**: activityService.log({userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata}). Activity repository wraps prisma.
14. **Repository pattern**: Only layer touching Prisma. Exports typed inputs from Prisma. findMany with where/skip/take/orderBy.
15. **Service pattern**: Business logic, calls repositories, generates codes (sequential via transaction), generates tokens, logs activity with actorId option, soft delete.
16. **Server actions**: "use server", getCurrentUser check, call service, return data.
17. **Video player**: Client component, full controls, modal overlay.
18. **Storage service**: StorageProvider interface, singleton, CloudflareR2Provider, methods: upload, replace, delete, exists, getSignedUrl, getDownloadUrl, getStreamingUrl, generateUploadUrl.
19. **Video grid**: Client component, useTransition, search/filter/sort/pagination, calls actions, uses EmptyState + ConfirmDialog + VideoPlayer.

21. **bcryptjs**: Available (v3.0.3) + @types/bcryptjs. DUMMY_HASH constant in config suggests bcrypt.compare usage. (Login action uses it — referenced.)

args.result:
## Overview I thoroughly explored the Photography Pixel Hub codebase (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, Prisma 7, PostgreSQL, RTL Arabic UI). All 24 requested files were read in full. Note: the `read_file` tool cached ~7 files as "unchanged since last read" from a prior context I couldn't see, so I recovered their contents via PowerShell `Get-Content -LiteralPath` (the bracketed paths `[id]`/`[token]` required `-LiteralPath` to avoid PowerShell wildcard errors). Below are the patterns most relevant to building a new Delivery Portal module. ## 1. Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`) **Enums:** `UserRole` (OWNER, ADMIN, EDITOR, MEDIA_BUYER, PHOTOGRAPHER, CLIENT), `ProjectStatus` (DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS, READY, DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), `RetentionPeriod` (TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS, FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS, SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS, SEVEN_DAYS, CUSTOM), `VideoStatus` (UPLOADING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, DELETED), `ActivityType` (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, VIEW, ARCHIVE, RESTORE, RETENTION_CHANGE), `ClientStatus` (ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED). **Models:** All use `String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid`, `createdAt @default(now())`, `updatedAt @updatedAt`. Key convention for public access: both `Client` and `Project` have a `token String @unique` field AND a sequential `clientCode`/`projectCode String @unique` field. Soft-delete via `archivedAt DateTime?`. `ActivityLog` (userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata Json?) provides the audit trail. `Download` and `View` are junction-style models tracking videoId/clientId/projectId + ip/userAgent. A new Delivery model should follow the same shape: uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status enum, relations to Project/Client, soft-delete `archivedAt`, and reuse `ActivityLog` for auditing. ## 2. Routes (`src/lib/constants/routes.ts`) `ROUTES` object with static strings + dynamic helpers like `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_DETAILS: (id) => /dashboard/projects/${id}` and `PUBLIC_GALLERY: (token) => /p/${token}`. `PROTECTED_ROUTES = ["/dashboard"]`, `AUTH_ROUTES = ["/login"]`, `API_ROUTES = { UPLOAD, WEBHOOKS }`. A delivery portal would add e.g. `PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token) => /d/${token}` and dashboard routes like `DASHBOARD_DELIVERIES`. ## 3. Navigation (`src/lib/constants/navigation.ts`) `NAV_SECTIONS: NavSection[]` (each `{title, items: NavItem[]}`), `NavItem = {label, href?, icon: LucideIcon, action?: "logout"}`. `NAV_FOOTER_ITEMS` for settings/profile/logout. `PAGE_TITLES` and `BREADCRUMB_LABELS` are `Record` keyed by path segment. New nav entries go in `NAV_SECTIONS`; add breadcrumb labels for any new segments. ## 4. Auth route group `src/app/(auth)/` `(auth)/layout.tsx` = centered full-screen layout (`min-h-dvh`, centered, APP_NAME heading). `(auth)/login/page.tsx` is a `"use client"` form using **react-hook-form + zodResolver(loginSchema)**, builds `FormData`, calls `loginAction(prevState, formData)` server action, handles `?redirect=` and `?reason=expired` query params via `getSafeRedirect()`, shows toasts (sonner). Pattern to reuse for any delivery login/gate page. ## 5. Public gallery route `src/app/p/[token]/` Contains `page.tsx`, `loading.tsx` (spinner), `error.tsx` (`"use client"`, `useEffect` console.error, reset button). **Critical pattern:** `page.tsx` is an `async` server component with `params: Promise<{ token: string }>`, awaits `const { token } = await params`. Currently a **stabilization stub** ("المعرض غير متاح حالياً") — no real gallery logic yet, so the Delivery Portal can establish the real token-based public-access pattern. This is the template for a `/d/[token]` delivery route. ## 6. Project details page (`src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/page.tsx`) Server component, `async params: Promise<{id}>`, calls `projectService.getById(id)` directly (not via action), `notFound()` if missing, fetches `getStatistics` + `activityService.list({entity, entityId, take})`. Uses `PageHeader` (with breadcrumbs + back button action), `DashboardCard`, `SectionTitle`, a local `StatCard` component, `ProjectStatusBadge`/`RetentionBadge`, `EmptyState`. Layout: status bar → 4-col stat grid → 2/3 + 1/3 split (info cards left, activity timeline right). The directory also has a `[id]/videos/page.tsx` subroute. **A "Deliveries" tab/section would slot into this page** alongside the Videos card, or as a new `[id]/deliveries` subroute. ## 7. Clients list page (`src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx`) Minimal: `export const metadata = { title }`, server component returning `
`. List pages are thin server shells delegating to a client `*Table` component (which owns search/filter/sort/pagination via server actions). ## 8. Shared components - **`page-header.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{title, description?, actions?, breadcrumbs?}`; auto-generates breadcrumbs from `usePathname()` using `BREADCRUMB_LABELS`. RTL-aware (`ChevronLeft` with `rtl-flip`). - **`empty-state.tsx`**: props `{icon: LucideIcon, title, description?, action?, className?, iconClassName?}` — circular muted icon + title + description + optional action. - **`confirm-dialog.tsx`** (`"use client"`): props `{open, onOpenChange, title, description, confirmLabel?, cancelLabel?, variant?: "default"|"destructive", onConfirm}`. Wraps Radix `Dialog` + `Button`. `onConfirm` awaited then closes. ## 9–10. Auth session & config `src/lib/auth/session.ts`: JWT via **jose** (Edge-compatible). `getCurrentUser()` → `SessionUser | null` (never throws). `requireUser()` throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ExpiredSessionError`. `requireRole(...allowedRoles: UserRole[])` throws `ForbiddenError`. `createSession(user)` signs + sets cookie; `destroySession()` deletes it. `SessionUser = {id, email, name, role, avatar}`. `src/lib/auth/config.ts`: cookie name `pph-session`, alg HS256, maxAge 7 days, `cookieOptions = {httpOnly, sameSite:"lax", secure: prod-only, path:"/", maxAge}`. Rate limit (5 attempts/min, 15-min lockout). **`DUMMY_HASH`** bcrypt constant for timing-attack prevention on missing users. ## 11–12. Utils `src/lib/utils/format.ts`: `formatBytes(bytes, decimals=2)`, `formatDate(date, locale="ar-SA", opts)` (Arabic long date), `formatDateTime`, `formatNumber` — all use `Intl` with `ar-SA`. `src/lib/utils/index.ts`: re-exports `cn`, format fns; **`generateToken(length=32)`** uses `crypto.getRandomValues` (browser+Node) over `[A-Za-z0-9]` — reuse for delivery tokens. Also `sleep(ms)`. ## 13–14. Activity logging (service + repository) `activityService.log({userId, type: ActivityType, entity, entityId, metadata?})` → `activityRepository.create(...)`. `activityService.list({userId?, type?, entity?, entityId?, skip?, take?})` builds a `where` and orders by `createdAt desc`. `activityRepository` is the ONLY Prisma-touching layer; exports `ActivityLogWithRelations`, `*CreateInput`, `*WhereInput` types from `Prisma.*`. Methods: `findById, create, findMany({where,skip,take,orderBy}), count`. ## 15. Server action pattern (`src/actions/projects/get-project-details.ts`) `"use server"`; `const user = await getCurrentUser(); if(!user) return null;` (graceful null, no throw); calls `projectService`; returns plain serializable object `{project, statistics, timeline}`. Actions are the server→client boundary; services hold logic. ## 16. Video player (`src/components/player/video-player.tsx`) `"use client"` full-screen modal overlay (`fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/90`). Props `{src, title?, poster?, onClose?, autoPlay?}`. Custom controls: play/pause, seek bar, volume/mute, playback speed (0.5–2x), fullscreen. Uses `useRef` + event listeners. Reusable for delivery video playback. ## 17. Storage service (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`) Facade pattern: `StorageProvider` interface + `CloudflareR2Provider` (active), swappable via `STORAGE_CONFIG.PROVIDER`. Singleton `storageService` exposes: `upload, replace, delete, exists, getSignedUrl, getDownloadUrl, getStreamingUrl, generateUploadUrl`. Re-exported types `StorageProvider, UploadParams, SignedUrlOptions`. Deliveries should use `getDownloadUrl`/`getStreamingUrl` for secure token-based file access. ## 18. Project types (`src/types/project.ts`) App-level types: `ProjectWithRelations`, `ProjectTableRow` (flat display row with nested `client`), `PaginatedProjects {items, total, page, pageSize, totalPages}`, `ProjectStatistics {videos, views, downloads, storageSize}`, `ProjectDetails`. Mirror this for `Delivery` types. ## 19. Validations (`src/lib/validations/project.ts`) Zod. Enum schemas (`projectStatusSchema`, `retentionPeriodSchema`, `projectFilterSchema`, `projectSortSchema`). `createProjectSchema`/`updateProjectSchema` (`z.object` with `nameSchema` from `./auth`, `z.coerce.date()`, Arabic `.max()` messages). `projectQuerySchema` with `z.coerce.number()` defaults + `refine` for page sizes `[10,25,50,100]`. Types via `z.infer`. Reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth` and the query-schema pattern for deliveries. ## 20. Repository pattern (`src/repositories/project.repository.ts`) ONLY Prisma layer. Exports `ProjectWithRelations = Prisma.ProjectGetPayload<{include:{client,videos}}>`, `*CreateInput`/`*UpdateInput` (Unchecked), `*WhereInput`. Private `buildWhere`/`buildOrderBy`/`statusFromFilter` helpers. Methods: `findById, findByProjectCode, findByToken, create, update, softDelete (status=ARCHIVED+archivedAt), restore, delete (hard, tests only), findMany (parallel findMany+count), count, findLatestProjectCode, getStatistics (parallel video sizes + view/download counts), findAllForExport`. **`findByToken`** is the key method to mirror for public delivery access. ## 21. Service pattern (`src/services/project.service.ts`) Business logic, calls repositories only. `generateUniqueProjectCode()` uses `prisma.$transaction` to read latest `projectCode`, parse `PR-(\d+)`, increment, format `PR-000001`. `normalizeInput` trims. `create` generates code+`generateToken(32)`, creates, then **conditional activity logging** via `options.actorId` (CREATE, and UPDATE logs RETENTION_CHANGE when changed). `archive`/`restore`/`delete` (alias). `list`/`count` map filter→status. `getTimeline` delegates to `activityService.list`. `exportCsv` builds Arabic CSV with BOM. Reuse the `{actorId}` options + sequential-code-via-transaction + token-generation pattern for deliveries. ## 22. Video grid (`src/components/uploads/video-grid.tsx`) `"use client"` grid with `useTransition`. State: `data, search, filter, sort, page, pageSize` + targets for delete/restore/player. `fetchData` calls `getUploadsAction`. **`useMemo` (not useEffect) triggers `fetchData`** on dependency change; debounced search via `setTimeout` in a `useMemo`. Toolbar: search input (RTL `ps-9`), filter tablist, sort select. Grid: `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`, skeleton loaders, `EmptyState`, status badges, thumbnail→`handlePlay` fetches signed URLs then opens `VideoPlayer`. Pagination with page-size select + ChevronRight/Left (RTL). `ConfirmDialog` for delete/restore with sonner toasts. Template for a delivery file grid. ## 23. bcryptjs — AVAILABLE ✓ `package.json`: `"bcryptjs": "^3.0.3"` (dependency) + `"@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6"` (devDependency). Ready for password-hashing on password-protected deliveries. Note: the `read_file` tool cached ~7 files as "unchanged since last read" from a prior context I couldn't see, so I recovered their contents via PowerShell `Get-Content -LiteralPath` (the bracketed paths `[id]`/`[token]` required `-LiteralPath` to avoid PowerShell wildcard errors). Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`) **Enums:** `UserRole` (OWNER, ADMIN, EDITOR, MEDIA_BUYER, PHOTOGRAPHER, CLIENT), `ProjectStatus` (DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS, READY, DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), `RetentionPeriod` (TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS, FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS, SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS, SEVEN_DAYS, CUSTOM), `VideoStatus` (UPLOADING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, DELETED), `ActivityType` (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, VIEW, ARCHIVE, RESTORE, RETENTION_CHANGE), `ClientStatus` (ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED). **Models:** All use `String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid`, `createdAt @default(now())`, `updatedAt @updatedAt`. Key convention for public access: both `Client` and `Project` have a `token String @unique` field AND a sequential `clientCode`/`projectCode String @unique` field. Soft-delete via `archivedAt DateTime?`. `ActivityLog` (userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata Json?) provides the audit trail. `Download` and `View` are junction-style models tracking videoId/clientId/projectId + ip/userAgent. A new Delivery model should follow the same shape: uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status enum, relations to Project/Client, soft-delete `archivedAt`, and reuse `ActivityLog` for auditing. ## 2. Routes (`src/lib/constants/routes.ts`) `ROUTES` object with static strings + dynamic helpers like `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_DETAILS: (id) => /dashboard/projects/${id}` and `PUBLIC_GALLERY: (token) => /p/${token}`. `PROTECTED_ROUTES = ["/dashboard"]`, `AUTH_ROUTES = ["/login"]`, `API_ROUTES = { UPLOAD, WEBHOOKS }`. A delivery portal would add e.g. `PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token) => /d/${token}` and dashboard routes like `DASHBOARD_DELIVERIES`. ## 3. Navigation (`src/lib/constants/navigation.ts`) `NAV_SECTIONS: NavSection[]` (each `{title, items: NavItem[]}`), `NavItem = {label, href?, icon: LucideIcon, action?: "logout"}`. `NAV_FOOTER_ITEMS` for settings/profile/logout. `PAGE_TITLES` and `BREADCRUMB_LABELS` are `Record` keyed by path segment. New nav entries go in `NAV_SECTIONS`; add breadcrumb labels for any new segments. ## 4. Auth route group `src/app/(auth)/` `(auth)/layout.tsx` = centered full-screen layout (`min-h-dvh`, centered, APP_NAME heading). `(auth)/login/page.tsx` is a `"use client"` form using **react-hook-form + zodResolver(loginSchema)**, builds `FormData`, calls `loginAction(prevState, formData)` server action, handles `?redirect=` and `?reason=expired` query params via `getSafeRedirect()`, shows toasts (sonner). Public gallery route `src/app/p/[token]/` Contains `page.tsx`, `loading.tsx` (spinner), `error.tsx` (`"use client"`, `useEffect` console.error, reset button). **Critical pattern:** `page.tsx` is an `async` server component with `params: Promise<{ token: string }>`, awaits `const { token } = await params`. Currently a **stabilization stub** ("المعرض غير متاح حالياً") — no real gallery logic yet, so the Delivery Portal can establish the real token-based public-access pattern. Project details page (`src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/page.tsx`) Server component, `async params: Promise<{id}>`, calls `projectService.getById(id)` directly (not via action), `notFound()` if missing, fetches `getStatistics` + `activityService.list({entity, entityId, take})`. Layout: status bar → 4-col stat grid → 2/3 + 1/3 split (info cards left, activity timeline right). Clients list page (`src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx`) Minimal: `export const metadata = { title }`, server component returning `
`. List pages are thin server shells delegating to a client `*Table` component (which owns search/filter/sort/pagination via server actions). ## 8. Server action pattern (`src/actions/projects/get-project-details.ts`) `"use server"`; `const user = await getCurrentUser(); if(!user) return null;` (graceful null, no throw); calls `projectService`; returns plain serializable object `{project, statistics, timeline}`. Actions are the server→client boundary; services hold logic. ## 16. Video player (`src/components/player/video-player.tsx`) `"use client"` full-screen modal overlay (`fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/90`). Props `{src, title?, poster?, onClose?, autoPlay?}`. Custom controls: play/pause, seek bar, volume/mute, playback speed (0.5–2x), fullscreen. Uses `useRef` + event listeners. Reusable for delivery video playback. ## 17. Enum schemas (`projectStatusSchema`, `retentionPeriodSchema`, `projectFilterSchema`, `projectSortSchema`). `createProjectSchema`/`updateProjectSchema` (`z.object` with `nameSchema` from `./auth`, `z.coerce.date()`, Arabic `.max()` messages). `projectQuerySchema` with `z.coerce.number()` defaults + `refine` for page sizes `[10,25,50,100]`. Types via `z.infer`. Reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth` and the query-schema pattern for deliveries. ## 20. Repository pattern (`src/repositories/project.repository.ts`) ONLY Prisma layer. Exports `ProjectWithRelations = Prisma.ProjectGetPayload<{include:{client,videos}}>`, `*CreateInput`/`*UpdateInput` (Unchecked), `*WhereInput`. Private `buildWhere`/`buildOrderBy`/`statusFromFilter` helpers. Methods: `findById, findByProjectCode, findByToken, create, update, softDelete (status=ARCHIVED+archivedAt), restore, delete (hard, tests only), findMany (parallel findMany+count), count, findLatestProjectCode, getStatistics (parallel video sizes + view/download counts), findAllForExport`. **`findByToken`** is the key method to mirror for public delivery access. ## 21. Service pattern (`src/services/project.service.ts`) Business logic, calls repositories only. `generateUniqueProjectCode()` uses `prisma.$transaction` to read latest `projectCode`, parse `PR-(\d+)`, increment, format `PR-000001`. `normalizeInput` trims. `create` generates code+`generateToken(32)`, creates, then **conditional activity logging** via `options.actorId` (CREATE, and UPDATE logs RETENTION_CHANGE when changed). `archive`/`restore`/`delete` (alias). `list`/`count` map filter→status. `getTimeline` delegates to `activityService.list`. `exportCsv` builds Arabic CSV with BOM. Reuse the `{actorId}` options + sequential-code-via-transaction + token-generation pattern for deliveries. ## 22. Video grid (`src/components/uploads/video-grid.tsx`) `"use client"` grid with `useTransition`. State: `data, search, filter, sort, page, pageSize` + targets for delete/restore/player. `fetchData` calls `getUploadsAction`. **`useMemo` (not useEffect) triggers `fetchData`** on dependency change; debounced search via `setTimeout` in a `useMemo`. Toolbar: search input (RTL `ps-9`), filter tablist, sort select. Grid: `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`, skeleton loaders, `EmptyState`, status badges, thumbnail→`handlePlay` fetches signed URLs then opens `VideoPlayer`. Pagination with page-size select + ChevronRight/Left (RTL). `ConfirmDialog` for delete/restore with sonner toasts. Template for a delivery file grid. ## 23. bcryptjs — AVAILABLE ✓ `package.json`: `"bcryptjs": "^3.0.3"` (dependency) + `"@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6"` (devDependency). Ready for password-hashing on password-protected deliveries. The `DUMMY_HASH` constant in `auth/config.ts` confirms the `bcrypt.compare` timing-attack pattern is already in use (likely in the login action). ## 24. Root layout (`src/app/layout.tsx`) ``, Cairo (Arabic) + Inter fonts. Providers nested: `ThemeProvider` (attribute="class", defaultTheme="dark", enableSystem) → `SessionProvider initialUser={user}` (user fetched server-side via `getCurrentUser()`) → `QueryProvider` (TanStack) → children + `Toaster` (sonner, top-center, richColors, rtl). `metadata` uses APP_NAME/APP_DESCRIPTION/APP_LOCALE/APP_URL from `@/lib/constants`. New public delivery routes inherit this layout automatically. ## Bonus findings - `middleware.ts` exists at project root (protects `/dashboard` per PROTECTED_ROUTES) — public `/d/[token]` routes must be excluded like `/p/[token]` and `/login`. - Project details dir has subroute `[id]/videos/page.tsx` — a `[id]/deliveries` subroute would follow the same convention. - The `(auth)` route group is a Next.js route group (parens, no URL segment) for shared centered layout — useful if deliveries need a separate gate/login layout. - `SessionProvider` receives `initialUser` from the server, so client components can read the current user without re-fetching. ## Architectural layering summary (for new Delivery module) `prisma/schema.prisma` → `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` (only Prisma layer) → `src/services/delivery.service.ts` (logic, token+code generation, activity logging via `{actorId}`) → `src/actions/deliveries/*.ts` (`"use server"`, `getCurrentUser` guard) → `src/components/deliveries/*` (client UI: table/grid/forms) → `src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/deliveries/page.tsx` (admin tab) + `src/app/d/[token]/page.tsx` (public portal). Validations in `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema`), types in `src/types/delivery.ts`, routes/nav in constants, and bcryptjs for any password-gated deliveries. Next steps: Build the Delivery Portal module following the established layered pattern: (1) Add `Delivery` model + `DeliveryStatus`/`DeliveryItem` enums to `prisma/schema.prisma` mirroring the Project shape (uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status, relations to Project/Client, `archivedAt` soft-delete). (2) Create `src/repositories/delivery.repository.ts` with `findByToken`/`findById`/`findMany`/`create`/`softDelete` mirroring project.repository. (3) Create `src/services/delivery.service.ts` with sequential-code transaction + `generateToken(32)` + `{actorId}` activity logging. (4) Add `src/lib/validations/delivery.ts` (Zod, reuse `nameSchema` from `./auth`) and `src/types/delivery.ts`. (5) Add routes to `routes.ts` (`PUBLIC_DELIVERY: (token)=>/d/${token}`, dashboard delivery routes) and a nav entry + breadcrumb labels in `navigation.ts`. (6) Ensure `middleware.ts` excludes the new `/d` public path (like `/p`). (7) Add server actions in `src/actions/deliveries/`, a `[id]/deliveries` admin tab under the project details page, and the public `/d/[token]/page.tsx` (with loading.tsx + error.tsx) reusing `VideoPlayer`, `storageService.getDownloadUrl/getStreamingUrl`, and bcryptjs for password-gated deliveries.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).

## Overview
I thoroughly explored the Photography Pixel Hub codebase (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, Prisma 7, PostgreSQL, RTL Arabic UI). All 24 requested files were read in full. Note: the `read_file` tool cached ~7 files as "unchanged since last read" from a prior context I couldn't see, so I recovered their contents via PowerShell `Get-Content -LiteralPath` (the bracketed paths `[id]`/`[token]` required `-LiteralPath` to avoid PowerShell wildcard errors). Below are the patterns most relevant to building a new Delivery Portal module.

## 1. Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`)
**Enums:** `UserRole` (OWNER, ADMIN, EDITOR, MEDIA_BUYER, PHOTOGRAPHER, CLIENT), `ProjectStatus` (DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS, READY, DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), `RetentionPeriod` (TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS, FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS, SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS, SEVEN_DAYS, CUSTOM), `VideoStatus` (UPLOADING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, DELETED), `ActivityType` (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, VIEW, ARCHIVE, RESTORE, RETENTION_CHANGE), `ClientStatus` (ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED).

**Models:** All use `String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid`, `createdAt @default(now())`, `updatedAt @updatedAt`. Key convention for public access: both `Client` and `Project` have a `token String @unique` field AND a sequential `clientCode`/`projectCode String @unique` field. Soft-delete via `archivedAt DateTime?`. `ActivityLog` (userId, type, entity, entityId, metadata Json?) provides the audit trail. `Download` and `View` are junction-style models tracking videoId/clientId/projectId + ip/userAgent. A new Delivery model should follow the same shape: uuid id, unique `token` + sequential `deliveryCode`, status enum, relations to Project/Client, soft-delete `archivedAt`, and reuse `ActivityLog` for auditing.

## 2. Routes (`src/lib/constants/routes.ts`)
`ROUTES` object with static strings + dynamic helpers like `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_DETAILS: (id) => /dashboard/projects/${id}` and `PUBLIC_GALLERY: (token) => /p/${
